property_scores.justification: Precise match to Education taxonomy value with strong institutional evidence (PSE listing, website self-description, 100+ year university history).
Clear vertical niche identified (STEM/engineering education, AI curriculum specialization) with supporting evidence, though broader than a single niche.
Market explicitly positioned at growth/consolidation phase with quantified TAM expansion (USD 154M to 847M by 2034) and regulatory consolidation drivers.
Tuition and enrollment-based model is implied but evidence is generic; no granular breakdown of revenue streams (research, partnerships, ancillary) provided.
Fixed-cost-heavy asset-intensive structure is precisely articulated with specific evidence (facilities, faculty, accreditation, capex commitments).
No profile_fill provided; moat analysis omitted entirely from manifest.
No profile_fill provided; dependencies on accreditation bodies, government regulation, and faculty labor market not assessed.
Exact headcount (1,895) from PSE filings with YoY growth tracking; precise match to Mid-Enterprise taxonomy band.
Revenue precisely quantified (PHP 5.32B FY2024 ≈ USD 95M) with YoY growth and TTM confirmation; exact Enterprise band match.
Public company status, majority ownership structure, and P22B capex plan are clearly documented; minor gap on specific share structure details.
Asset-heavy classification precisely supported with multi-campus infrastructure, expansion projects, and capex commitments across 4+ regions.
Profitable with strong growth (revenue +18%, earnings +31%), positive cash flow, conservative leverage; evidence comprehensive except minor ROIC context missing.
Legacy status indisputable (Mapúa founded 1925, centennial celebrated; iPeople holding for decades) with institutional continuity evidence.
Precise customer count (74,000 enrolled students) with YoY growth rate (10%) and geographic/institutional disaggregation; unambiguous Large band.
Growth phase is marked with empty placeholder object ({}); 10% YoY enrollment growth and facility expansion confirm scaling, but assessment is skeletal.
No profile_fill provided; student/consumer customer segment is implicit but not explicitly profiled.
Market leader position in STEM private HE is strongly evidenced (ABET, QS ranking, first-mover initiatives); rollup strategy context adds depth.
Brand/reputation-led motion is clear with supporting evidence (no outbound sales, partnership distribution), but GTM assessment is limited to inbound patterns.
Highly diversified revenue across 74,000 individual student accounts is precisely articulated; no concentration risk detected.
Dual-tier pricing (Mapúa premium + NTC/APEC affordable) is clearly described; evidence relies on positioning statements rather than absolute price data.
Dual professional management structure (Dr. Reynaldo Vea + Fred Ayala) representing conglomerate shareholders is well-documented; succession/personal depth limited.
Divisional holding company structure with semi-autonomous subsidiaries is precisely outlined; hub-and-spoke model confirmed with institutional subsidiaries identified.
Multi-site co-located model is explicitly stated and inherent to education delivery; campus presence across 4+ regions clearly documented.
Domain-expert-heavy (faculty) and operations-heavy (administration) composition is articulated; tech talent capability gap identified but not quantified.
Transitional architecture is detailed with specific platform names (Moodle, Blackboard, CARDINAL, Coursera integration); overall assessment confident but future direction unclear.
Minimal-to-basic practices appropriately assessed for a non-tech company; IT as operational support is clear, but internal development maturity not detailed.
Basic-to-intermediate data maturity identified with CARDINAL RFID and Coursera analytics, but data warehouse/BI platform status unknown; confidence low.
Basic-to-intermediate security posture inferred from regulatory requirements (Data Privacy Act, QM certification) but no direct evidence of SOC2/ISO27001 or incident response maturity.
Heavy regulatory burden is precisely characterized with four distinct regulators (CHED, DepEd, PSE/SEC, ABET, NPC) and active compliance context.
Single-country national scope is unambiguous (Philippines only, 4+ regions) with international partnership reach clearly delineated as non-operational.
Recession-resistant classification is justified with demographic and essential-service arguments; forex exposure identified but not quantified.
Public company and dual conglomerate ownership structure is thoroughly documented; governance complexity articulated; minority shareholder protections noted.
Fast-follower-to-bleeding-edge positioning (relative to Philippine HE) is supported with first-mover examples (BS AI, ABET, QM certification); institutional constraints acknowledged.
Dual brand personality (thought-leader Mapúa + mission-driven affordable segment) is clearly articulated with specific positioning evidence from website and messaging.
Values-prominent classification is well-supported with public statements, sustainability commitment evidence, and actual 54% affordable enrollment alignment.
Strong-in-niche employer assessment supported by Ayala/Yuchengco pedigree and education sector reputation; tech talent gap noted but confidence moderated by limited Glassdoor/survey data.
